title: Flowise chatflow update endpoint mass-assigns request body, allowing cross-workspace reassignment and deploy/visibility tampering
description: In the updateChatflow handler of packages/server/src/controllers/chatflows/index.ts (PUT /api/v1/chatflows/{id}), the entire JSON request body (req.body) is copied wholesale into a new ChatFlow entity via Object.assign(updateChatFlow, body), which is then merged onto the fetched chatflow and saved by chatflowsService.updateChatflow. The chatflow is loaded scoped to the caller's activeWorkspaceId, but no field restriction is applied, so attacker-supplied server-controlled properties such as workspaceId, deployed, isPublic, createdDate and updatedDate are merged into and persisted on the entity. An authenticated user can set workspaceId to an arbitrary workspace UUID to reassign the chatflow out of their tenant (and flip deployed/isPublic), breaking workspace isolation.
